facts.text: Ithaca takes its name from the Greek island that inspired Odysseus, echoing the classical roots of many upstate New York towns.
Cornell University, founded in 1865 by Ezra Cornell, sits on East Hill overlooking the town and shapes Ithaca’s identity.
In 1883, William Henry Baker launched the Ithaca Gun Company, turning the city into a historic shotgun‑making center.
Moosewood Restaurant began as a 1973 collective, publishing vegetarian cookbooks that spread Ithaca’s culinary reputation worldwide.
The Sciencenter, opened in 1983, offers hands‑on science exhibits and earned accreditation from the American Alliance of Museums.
Ithaca Journal, established in 1815, has been the town’s daily newspaper since the early 19th century and joined Gannett in 1912.
